title: Ginger Chicken Stir-Fry with Broccoli
steps: Cook jasmine rice according to package directions and set aside.
Slice chicken breast into thin strips and season with a pinch of salt.
Mince garlic and grate ginger root finely.
Heat sesame oil in a wok over high heat until shimmering.
Stir-fry chicken strips for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add bell peppers and broccoli florets. Stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return chicken to the wok, add soy sauce, and toss everything together for 1 minute.
reasoning: This recipe prioritizes your chicken breast (expires tomorrow) and ginger root (expires in 2 days) as the star ingredients. The stir-fry technique is quick and brings out the ginger's brightness. Bell peppers and broccoli add color, crunch, and essential vitamins. The dish uses 7 of your 10 inventory items, minimizing waste. Only sesame oil needs purchasing — everything else comes from your fridge.
nutritional_gaps: Your inventory is low on leafy greens (no spinach, kale, or lettuce) and healthy fats (no avocado, nuts, or olive oil). Consider adding a side salad for fiber.
